page: Amazon Web Services f2.12xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. This configuration delivers 48 virtual CPUs across 24 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The system features 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 10.67 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided by a bundled 1880 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps. The server runs on the Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the f2.12xlarge demonstrates strong CPU capabilities, placing in the top 25% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, as well as the PassMark CPU and encryption tests. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core, ray tracing, and image processing also rank in the top 25%. Conversely, memory bandwidth for both read and write operations falls within the average range, as do Redis SET operations, static web serving throughput, and software compilation. Large-model LLM inference text generation for a 70B model is a notable performance bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 10%.

From a resource density perspective, the server offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of over 10 GB per vCPU alongside fast local NVMe storage, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require GPU acceleration. However, the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training. Appropriate workloads include memory-intensive database operations, multi-threaded data decompression, image processing, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring significant local storage and memory capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services f2.12xlarge is an x86_64 server featuring a dedicated 48-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, paired with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a bundled 1880 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 25 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core CPU performance, PassMark encryption, and multi-threaded decompression, all placing in the top 25%. However, memory bandwidth and database operations remain in the average tier, and text generation for large 70B LLM models is poor, ranking in the bottom 10%. With a high memory-to-core ratio and no GPU, this server represents a qualitatively cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy workloads and local storage operations. It is suited for multi-threaded data processing, image processing, and memory-intensive database applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services f2.12xlarge is an x86_64 server with 48 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 1880 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core CPU performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services f2.12xlarge features 48 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 1880 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 server combining forty-eight vCPUs, half a terabyte of DDR4 memory, and fast local NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
512.0 GB of DDR4 memory paired with 1880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core CPU, ray tracing, and encryption benchmark performance
Baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for memory-intensive databases, image processing, and multi-threaded decompression
